data.title: ​​Merck Stock (MRK) Looks Bullish — Is the Market Underestimating the Post-Keytruda Playbook?
data.description:
Merck (MRK) stock looks bullish, with the market likely underestimating the strength of its post-Keytruda strategy. Investors remain anchored on the late-2028 Keytruda patent cliff, with less focus on the company's increasingly credible post-Keytruda playbook — an expanded pipeline across oncology and specialty drugs to drive the next phase of growth. Assets like Winrevair, belzutifan, and enlicitide, alongside a broader late-stage pipeline, support earnings expansion and potential multiple re-rating, reinforcing a positive outlook.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As one of the world's largest pharmaceutical companies, Merck derives its strength from blockbuster franchises in oncology, vaccines, and specialty medicine. The stock is up about 40% over the past six months, handily outperforming the S&P 500's (SPX) roughly 1.3% decline over the same period.

The Market Still Sees Keytruda First

The central debate around Merck remains the same: what happens after Keytruda loses exclusivity? That concern is understandable. Keytruda is one of the most important oncology drugs in the industry, and any company facing a cliff of that size will attract skepticism.

However, I think the market is making two mistakes. First, it is treating the Keytruda loss of exclusivity as if Merck has no credible transition strategy. Second, it is assuming that the stock's recent rebound means the post-Keytruda story is already fully priced in. I do not think either is true.

What has changed over the last few months is not just sentiment, but visibility. Merck is no longer asking investors to simply trust a distant pipeline. It now has a series of near-term launches, pivotal readouts, and commercial ramps that can begin to reshape estimates well before 2028 arrives.

Winrevair Could Be Bigger Than Investors Think

One of the most important pieces of that transition story is Winrevair. The market already recognizes its value in pulmonary arterial hypertension, where the drug is annualizing at roughly $1.87 billion, with Q4 sales at $467 million, and where 2030 revenue expectations already sit in the multi-billion-dollar range.

Now, the next leg of upside could come from CADENCE, a clinical trial evaluating Winrevair's efficacy in combined post- and precapillary pulmonary hypertension (PH) due to heart failure with preserved ejection fraction. The patient population enrolled in CADENCE may be roughly as large as the current pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH) market in the United States. If proven effective, the total addressable market expands to include the larger, more prevalent Group 2 PH patients.

Thus, Winrevair's long-term revenue potential rises meaningfully, reinforcing the idea that Merck's non-Keytruda growth engine is stronger than the market currently models.

The Pipeline Has More Ways to Surprise

Merck's post-Keytruda case does not rest on Winrevair alone. In fact, one reason I am constructive is that the company now has multiple shots on goal in 2026. Enlicitide, the oral PCSK9 inhibitor, is one of the most interesting near-term opportunities because it offers the potential for a first-in-class launch in a large cardiovascular market.

Belzutifan also looks increasingly underappreciated. Its growth in renal cell carcinoma could be much larger than consensus expects, especially as it moves into earlier lines and new combinations. Merck already reported more than $600 million in belzutifan sales in the U.S. in 2025, and the case for significant expansion from here looks strong.

Then there is Sacituzumab Tirumotecan (sac-TMT), which many increasingly see as a potentially best-in-class trophoblast cell-surface antigen 2 (TROP2) antibody-drug conjugate. If the upcoming lung and breast cancer data continue to look differentiated — particularly on safety and durability — this program could become one of the more important long-term oncology assets in the portfolio. Add in TNF-like cytokine 1A (TL1A), Welireg expansion, Ohtuvayre, and the broader immunology and oncology development engine, and the picture starts to look very different from the simple "Keytruda cliff" narrative.

Merck's Recovery Could Start Before the Cliff Arrives

One of the more compelling parts of the bullish case is that Merck may not need to wait until after the Keytruda loss of exclusivity to re-rate higher. That is because stocks usually do not wait for the cliff to pass before they recover. They begin re-rating when investors gain confidence that the company can navigate the trough and return to growth. I think Merck may already be in the early stages of that process.

There is a useful precedent here in how other large pharma names traded through major loss-of-exclusivity periods. Multiples often bottom when fear is highest, then recover as visibility improves. Merck was trading at depressed levels relative to its own history and to peers because investors were heavily discounting the Keytruda issue. Now that pipeline visibility is improving, the market may begin to pay a higher multiple before 2028 arrives.

That is particularly important because Merck still has significant financial flexibility. Management has the balance sheet, cash generation, and strategic room to continue investing in R&D and pursue acquisitions if attractive opportunities emerge. That flexibility should matter more as the company builds a bridge from Keytruda dominance to a broader portfolio.

Wall Street's View

According to TipRanks, Merck has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating, with 13 Buy, six Hold, and no Sell ratings. Based on 19 Wall Street analysts, the average price target is $131.42, implying about 12.9% upside from the recent share price of $116.37.
data.author: Davit Kirakosyan

data.title: "Emerging AI Powerhouse": Wedbush's Ives Doubles Down on Tesla's Terafab Push
data.description:
Wedbush top analyst Daniel Ives remains highly optimistic about Tesla's (TSLA) future as an "AI powerhouse," reiterating his Buy rating and Street-high price target of $600 (56.6% upside potential). This comes amid bold announcements from CEO Elon Musk highlighting Tesla's push into advanced semiconductor manufacturing to fuel its AI-driven future.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade TSLA with leverage
  • New trading tool for TSLA bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade TSLA

Ives is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #717 out of 12,128 analysts tracked. He has a 51% success rate and an average return per rating of 11.30%.

Roadmap to an AI-Driven Future

CEO Elon Musk announced that SpaceX and Tesla will build two advanced chip factories in Austin, Texas. The companies are prioritizing a Terafab facility, a massive semiconductor fabrication plant capable of producing enormous volumes of chips, to meet surging demand for the chip technology powering Tesla's AI strategy.

One factory will supply chips for Tesla's electric vehicles (EVs) and Optimus humanoid robots, while the other targets AI data centers, including those in space. Musk aims to generate 1 terawatt of capacity annually, roughly double the current U.S. production.

Ives highlighted that existing chip suppliers like Micron (MU), Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM), and Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) will be unable to keep pace with Tesla's AI needs.

Although the project timeline remains unclear, Ives views this as a game-changer. The move will accelerate Tesla's AI roadmap, positioning the company as an AI powerhouse in the years ahead. Ives acknowledged that securing chip and memory supply is poised to be Tesla's biggest bottleneck, and this initiative addresses it head-on. He also sees it as the first step toward a potential Tesla-SpaceX merger, likely in 2027, combining their strengths in AI, robotics, and space tech.

In related news, The Information reported that SpaceX is planning to file its initial public offering (IPO) prospectus with regulators as early as this week or next. The company could raise over $75 billion at a $1.25 trillion valuation, with a public listing targeted as soon as June.

Is Tesla a Good Buy at the Moment?

Not all analysts share Ives' bullishness. On TipRanks, TSLA has a Hold consensus rating based on 13 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $399.25 implies 4.2% upside potential from current levels. Year-to-date, TSLA shares have fallen 14.8%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Palantir Stock (PLTR) Rises, Joins Anduril in Building Software for $185B Golden Dome Missile Shield Project
data.description:
Data analytics company Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and privately-held defense technology company Anduril Industries are part of the consortium that is developing software for U.S. President Donald Trump's planned $185 billion Golden Dome antimissile shield project, the Wall Street Journal reported. PLTR stock was up 1.25% in Tuesday's after-hours trading.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Palantir stock has declined 13% year-to-date, but has risen about 60% over the past year.

Palantir and Anduril Are Working on the Software for the Golden Dome Project

The crucial Golden Dome project aims to build a space-based shield that can intercept ballistic, cruise, and hypersonic missiles. Palantir and Anduril are reportedly part of a consortium of defense and tech companies working on the Golden Dome software, with the aim of testing it this summer.

Palantir and Anduril haven't been publicly named for this project. Interestingly, Reuters reported last year that the two companies were collaborating with Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk's SpaceX on various parts of this key project. Other companies that are part of the group working on the project include networking company Aalyria Technologies, AI startup Scale AI, and software firm Swoop Technologies.

If the software delivers the desired results, its developers, such as Palantir, could secure a potential opportunity to earn billions of dollars for maintaining the system. Last week, the project's director, General Michael Guetlein, said that the price tag of the Golden Dome project has increased by $10 billion to $185 billion due to efforts to ramp up crucial space-based capabilities. It is worth noting that Lockheed Martin (LMT), RTX (RTX), and Northrop Grumman (NOC) had ​earlier ⁠joined the program as prime contractors.

Interestingly, while Palantir's Commercial division has been growing at an impressive rate, the Government unit continues to be a significant contributor to the overall revenue. In 2025, 54% of the overall $4.5 billion revenue came from customers in the Government segment, while the Commercial segment contributed 46%.

Is PLTR a Good Stock to Buy?

Currently, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Palantir Technologies stock based on 14 Buys, four Holds, and two Sell recommendations. The average PLTR stock price target of $194.61 indicates about 26% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Alibaba's Stock (BABA) Selloff Appears Driven by Costs, Not Demand
data.description:
Alibaba (BABA) posted a weak December quarter, but the challenge was more about costs than demand. Margins collapsed under the weight of aggressive investments in artificial intelligence (AI), cloud, and quick commerce expansion, signaling a broader reset of the company's cost structure and capital allocation. While cloud and AI scaling support long-term value creation, there are also execution risks and a longer path to margin recovery. Even with a seemingly "de-risked" valuation at current levels, I maintain a more cautious Hold rating.

data.author: Bernard Zambonin

data.title: Merck Chases $6B Terns Pharma Deal to Boost Cancer Pipeline — Is Keytruda's Expiry Driving the Panic?
data.description:
U.S.-based Merck & Co. (MRK) is reportedly nearing a $6 billion all-cash deal to acquire biotech company Terns Pharmaceuticals (TERN) as part of its plan to strengthen its drug pipeline ahead of Keytruda's patent expiration in 2028. Notably, Keytruda (pembrolizumab) is Merck's blockbuster immunotherapy drug and one of the world's best-selling medicines, widely used to treat various cancers. The Financial Times reported that the talks are at an advanced stage and could close in the coming days.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Merck develops medicines, vaccines, and biologics, with a focus on oncology and hospital products. Meanwhile, Terns Pharmaceuticals is a biotech company working on new medicines for liver disease (NASH), obesity, and cancer.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Gain on Hopes of Progress Toward Middle East Peace
data.description:
U.S. stock futures inched higher on Tuesday evening as optimism grew over potential de‑escalation in the Middle East following reports that the U.S. has proposed a peace plan to Iran. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.79%, 0.63%, and 0.61%, respectively, at 6:34 p.m. EDT on March 24.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Major stock indices declined in the regular trading session as oil prices climbed again. Also, markets pulled back after Iranian officials denied holding direct peace talks with the U.S., reversing gains driven by President Trump's earlier claims of "productive conversations." The Nasdaq, the Dow, and the S&P 500 dropped 0.8%, 0.2%, and 0.4%, respectively.

On the earnings front, Chewy (CHWY) and Paychex (PAYX) are scheduled to report quarterly numbers tomorrow.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Cathie Wood Pours $16M into Circle Stock, Dumps ROKU, TSM, TWST, BLSH
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made interesting portfolio adjustments on Tuesday, March 24, as shown in ARK's daily fund disclosures. The firm bought $16.3 million of Circle Internet (CRCL), while trimming stakes in Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM), Twist Bioscience (TWST), Roku (ROKU), and Bullish (BLSH). These trades align with Wood's innovation-focused strategy, targeting disruptive tech in a volatile market.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wood 'Buys the Dip' in Circle

The ARK Innovation Fund (ARKK), the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW), and the ARK Fintech Innovation Fund (ARKF) acquired 161,513 shares of stablecoin (USDC-USD) issuer Circle Internet for $16.3 million. Wood capitalized on a 20.1% dip in Circle shares triggered by regulators eyeing reduced yields on stablecoin holdings.

A revised Clarity Act bill proposes limiting issuers like Circle from offering yields on USDC holdings, restricting rewards to activity-based uses like payments, trading, or lending. This could erode stablecoins' appeal, which relies on bank-like interest rates. Wood, a longtime Circle bull, has bought on every dip and remains unfazed, betting on USDC's long-term potential.

Is CRCL a Buy or Hold?

On TipRanks, Circle stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 11 Buys, six Holds, and one Sell rating. The average Circle Internet price target of $131 implies 29.5% upside potential from current levels. Year-to-date, CRCL shares have gained 27.6%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Meta Found Guilty in Child Exploitation Suit – Fined $375M by New Mexico
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) was found guilty in a long-standing child exploitation lawsuit in New Mexico and ordered to pay $375 million for violating the state's children's safety law. A jury ruled that Meta's social media platforms, including Facebook and Instagram, are harmful to children's mental health. Meta was found liable for failing to protect kids from sexually explicit content, solicitation, and human trafficking. META stock fell 1.9% on the news yesterday.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The jury found Meta deceived users into believing its platforms were safe, endangering children and breaking consumer protection laws. The jury hit Meta with the maximum fine per violation, totaling $375 million.

Meta Rejects Verdict, Vows to Appeal

Meta rejected the verdict, insisting the company works hard to keep people safe on its platforms. A spokesperson acknowledged "challenges of identifying and removing bad actors or harmful content." The company will continue to defend itself strongly and remains confident in its record of protecting teens.

During the weeks-long trial, New Mexico prosecutors showed internal Meta documents and testimony proving the company ignored warnings about platform dangers. They argued that features let pedophiles target kids and addict them on purpose. Meta denied the allegations, saying it has spent years investing in safety over growth.

In 2023, New Mexico AG Raúl Torrez sued Meta after an undercover op. They created a fake profile for a 13-year-old girl, which Torrez said was flooded with images and solicitations from child abusers.

A Historic Win Against Big Tech

Torrez called the verdict "a historic victory for every child and family who has paid the price for Meta's choice to put profits over kids' safety." The case is one of many similar lawsuits by state AGs against Meta, Snap (SNAP), YouTube (GOOGL), and TikTok.

In Los Angeles, a jury is deciding a similar case where a young woman claims Meta and others made addictive apps that harmed her mental health. That suit is part of thousands of individual suits in California court. Plus, over 2,000 lawsuits from people and schools are pending in federal court.

Is META a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, META has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 40 Buys and five Hold ratings. The average Meta Platforms price target of $865.58 implies nearly 46% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, META shares have fallen 5.3%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: SpaceX IPO Filing Expected This Week — Should You Buy on Day One?
data.description:
Elon Musk's SpaceX is reportedly aiming to file its initial public offering (IPO) prospectus with regulators as early as this week or next. According to The Information, the company could raise more than $75 billion at a valuation of $1.25 trillion. The report also added that SpaceX is targeting a public listing as soon as June.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SpaceX's potential IPO in June is attracting strong interest as one of the most sought-after private companies, with fast growth in satellites (Starlink) and launch services. A possible $75B+ raise also points to strong demand from big investors. However, buying on day one can be risky due to hype-driven pricing, limited financial details, and high volatility. For most investors, it may be smarter to wait for the stock to settle and invest gradually for better long-term returns.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Micron Stock (MU) Slides as Rival SK Hynix Eyes a U.S. Listing
data.description:
Micron (MU) stock dipped on Tuesday alongside news that rival memory and storage company SK Hynix (HXSCL) is seeking additional funding. According to recent reports, SK Hynix wants to raise $10 billion from a listing in the U.S. This would help the company with its plan to purchase $8 billion of extreme ultraviolet equipment from ASML Holding (ASML) by the end of 2027.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

If SK Hynix pursues a U.S. listing for its shares, it could be a negative catalyst for Micron stock. As it stands now, investors don't have many options for investing in SK Hynix. The company's stock is only available over-the-counter or through the CSOP SK Hynix Daily 2X Leveraged Product ETF (HK:7709). However, a U.S. listing would make it more easily accessible to investors, who could trade out their shares of MU stock for SK Hynix stock.

Despite the potential increase in competition for Micron, the company did get a vote of investor confidence yesterday. Four-star Phillip Securities analyst Yik Ban Chong reiterated a Buy rating and increased his price target to $530 from $500, implying a possible 35.83% upside for the shares. Chong pointed to a strong earnings report and increased demand for its advanced high‑bandwidth memory for AI GPUs as reasons to remain bullish on MU stock.

Micron Stock Slips Today

Micron stock was down 2.55% on Tuesday but was still up 38.15% year-to-date. The stock has also rallied 329.34% over the past 12 months. Those year-over-year gains make sense, as the company has seen its shares boosted by the need for computer components amid the AI boom.

MU stock trading activity today was low, with about 4.45 million shares traded, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 35.7 million shares.
data.author: William White
